target: Percolation Threshold Theory x T Cell Infiltration in Solid Tumors

cross_domain_bonus.reason: All hypotheses bridge statistical mechanics (bond percolation theory) to tumor immunology — spanning physics and life sciences, constituting 2+ disciplinary boundaries

scoring_notes.dw_correction: Critic identified pipeline error: d_w = 3.8 (Alexander-Orbach conjecture, valid only for d >= 19) corrected to d_w = 2.878 (numerically measured for 3D percolation). MSD exponent changes from 0.53 to 0.695. Applied in Mechanistic Specificity and Groundedness scores for H1, H2, H3, H6.
scoring_notes.groundedness_floor: Critic groundedness scores used as floor for Groundedness dimension. Ranker may raise but not lower below Critic assessment.
scoring_notes.ashworth_prior_art: Ashworth 2015 (percolation + fibroblast invasion in collagen scaffolds) is acknowledged partial prior art for H1, H4. Does not constitute prior art for T cell tumor immunology application.

hypotheses.title: LOX Crosslink Density as Bond Occupation Probability Predicts a Universal Immune Exclusion Threshold at p ~ 0.21-0.25
hypotheses.evolution_notes: Core hypothesis that validates the percolation framework. Correct d_w to 2.878 (alpha = 0.695). Consider crossover with H5 to produce unified threshold + dose-response variant.

hypotheses.title: LOX Inhibitor Dose-Response Follows the Order Parameter Scaling P_inf ~ (p - p_c)^0.417
hypotheses.evolution_notes: Fix factual imprecision: PMID 39101793 uses PXS-5505 (pan-LOX inhibitor), not LOX-IN-3. Address multi-LOX-family confound in evolved variant. Consider crossover with H1.

hypotheses.title: Correlation Length Exponent nu = 0.88 Predicts T Cell Cluster Size Distribution Near the Hot-Cold Tumor Boundary
hypotheses.evolution_notes: Correct tau from 2.19 to 2.31 (measured value, not approximate formula). Address lattice constant ambiguity with explicit sensitivity analysis. Highest testability score — prioritize computational validation path.

hypotheses.title: Collagen I/III Ratio Acts as a Lattice Topology Switch That Shifts p_c
hypotheses.evolution_notes: Fix title to accurately describe 'p_c shift within same universality class' rather than 'universality class switch'. Obtain anisotropic percolation simulations or published values for p_c of fiber-aligned networks to replace the parametric 0.35-0.45 range.

hypotheses.title: Finite-Size Scaling of T Cell MSD Explains Discordant Infiltration Scores Between Core Biopsies and Resection Specimens
hypotheses.evolution_notes: Not selected for evolution: Elo tournament reveals that dominant alternative explanations (molecular heterogeneity) undermine unambiguous interpretability. Not selected despite tied linear score with H4 because H4 has a stronger biological anchor (Fusilier 2025) and cleaner experimental design.

hypotheses.title: TGF-beta Autocrine Signaling Constitutes Bond-Correlated Percolation with Non-Linear LOX/Anti-TGF-beta Synergy
hypotheses.evolution_notes: Selected for evolution because it represents the only correlated percolation mechanism (family 5) and has high translational impact. Evolution must: (1) correct TGF-beta effective range from 50-100 um to ~10-30 um (contact-dependent activation); (2) resolve direction of p_c shift under positive correlation; (3) replace Prakash 1992 with verified citation. High clinical potential justifies evolution despite Elo last place.

hypotheses.title: T Cell MSD Superdiffusive-to-Subdiffusive Transition with Self-Trapping Amplification
hypotheses.evolution_notes: Not selected for primary evolution. The Pe ~ 3 vs Pe >> 10 quantitative mismatch and T cell deformability concerns are fundamental obstacles. Could be revisited if 3D active percolation simulations at Pe ~ 3 become available. The frontier confirmation (arXiv 2603.04602) makes this a promising longer-term target.

diversity_check.redundancy_note: H1 and H5 share the same bridge family but address distinct predictions (threshold existence vs. dose-response curve shape). H2 and H3 share correlation length family but are computational vs. clinical. No adjustment needed.

elo_tournament.ranking_agreement: CONFIRMS — Elo and linear ranking agree on H1, H5, H2 as top 3
elo_tournament.divergence: H3 and H4 tied linearly at 7.10; Elo resolves tie in favor of H4 (rank 4) over H3 (rank 5)
elo_tournament.divergence_explanation: Elo captures interpretability advantage: H3 faces a dominant competing explanation (molecular heterogeneity) making results harder to interpret unambiguously; H4's prediction is more cleanly falsifiable within the percolation framework

skip_threshold_check.recommendation: Evolver should run — meaningful room for improvement via correction of d_w error, tau exponent, TGF-beta range, and H1+H5 crossover

evolver_instructions: Correct d_w = 3.8 to d_w = 2.878 (alpha = 0.695) in all evolved variants of H1, H2, H3
Correct tau from 2.19 to 2.31 (measured value) in H2 variants
Fix H5: replace LOX-IN-3 reference with PXS-5505 (PMID 39101793)
Fix H8: correct TGF-beta effective range from 50-100 um to ~10-30 um (contact-dependent activation via integrin alpha-v-beta-6)
Consider crossover: H1 x H5 → unified hypothesis that predicts both threshold existence AND dose-response shape
Fix H4 title: 'p_c shift within same universality class' not 'universality class switch'
